source: ATLAS V5 Director Profiles - Based on Actor Template Structure

directors.name: Helena Blackwood

visual_signature.camera_philosophy: The camera is a character. It breathes, hesitates, reveals.
visual_signature.composition_style: Negative space dominates, figures framed within architecture

movement_vocabulary.default: slow dolly, creep push, static dread

lighting_signature.philosophy: Motivated practical. If there's no source, there's no light.
lighting_signature.palette: candlelight amber, moonlight blue, never mixed artificially
lighting_signature.ratio: High contrast 8:1, deep shadows, faces half-lit

directors.default_camera_motion: locked tripod with subtle creep, weighted atmospheric stillness

presence.set_demeanor: Quiet intensity, speaks rarely but precisely

sample_direction: Let the silence work.
Find the dread in the stillness.
The house is watching. Show me that.
Don't act scared. Be someone who refuses to admit they're scared.

creative_philosophy.origin: Trained at RADA in theater, transitioned to film seeking 'permanent dread'
creative_philosophy.core_belief: Horror is not about what we see. It's about what we almost see.
creative_philosophy.methodology: Extensive rehearsal period. Actors must live in the space before cameras roll.
creative_philosophy.signature_technique: The 'Blackwood Hold' - never cut before the actor breaks

horror_beat.default: static wide, subject small in frame

revelation.default: slow push to CU, rack focus

directors.name: Victor Tanaka

visual_signature.camera_philosophy: The camera fights alongside the actors. Every frame is impact.
visual_signature.composition_style: Tight framing during combat, wide for choreography appreciation

movement_vocabulary.combat: Steadicam tracking, whip pans on impacts

lighting_signature.philosophy: High contrast to sculpt muscles, dramatic backlighting
lighting_signature.palette: High energy, motivated practicals plus stylized enhancement

directors.default_camera_motion: fluid tracking with whip pan transitions, impact shake
sample_direction: The punch lands in the edit. Give me the wind-up and recovery.
Fight like you're exhausted. This is round 10.
The space between the hits is where the audience breathes.
Make it hurt. Not look like it hurts. Actually hurt.

creative_philosophy.origin: Stunt performer turned director, learned filmmaking through physical storytelling
creative_philosophy.core_belief: Action is character. How someone fights tells you who they are.
creative_philosophy.methodology: Rehearse fights like dance numbers. Camera learns the choreography.
creative_philosophy.signature_technique: The 'Tanaka Oner' - one continuous shot through entire fight sequence

establishing.default: wide tracking, environment reveals threat geography

dialogue.default: tight two-shot, actors in same frame — no singles until tension breaks

action_beat.default: wide master for choreography, cut to medium for impacts

tension.default: slow push into confrontation, OTS favoring aggressor

climax.default: cross-cutting wide choreography and CU impacts

aftermath.default: static wide hold, let exhaustion play

directors.name: Claire Fontaine

visual_signature.camera_philosophy: Observe history. Don't intrude upon it.
visual_signature.composition_style: Painterly frames, classical composition, golden ratio

movement_vocabulary.default: elegant dolly, motivated by character movement
movement_vocabulary.tension: static locked frames, characters move within

lighting_signature.philosophy: Natural and available light, period-appropriate sources only
lighting_signature.palette: Warm amber daylight, cool blue moonlight, candle glow

directors.default_camera_motion: elegant dolly motivated by emotion, period-appropriate restraint
sample_direction: You're not in a costume. You live in these clothes.
Slower. People in 1840 weren't in a hurry.
The corset informs every breath. Let me see that.
Your hands tell the story of your station.

creative_philosophy.origin: Art history scholar turned filmmaker, treats each frame as a painting
creative_philosophy.core_belief: History speaks through gesture, posture, and the weight of fabric.
creative_philosophy.methodology: Three weeks of etiquette training before cameras roll. Every movement is period-accurate.
creative_philosophy.signature_technique: The 'Fontaine Tableau' - characters arranged like portraiture, camera observes

establishing.motion: elegant slow dolly, golden ratio composition

dialogue.default: medium portrait at 75mm, period-flattering soft focus

confession.default: slow push to CU during emotional reveals

spectacle.default: crane wide for scale, crowd as living painting

intimate.lighting: warm candle glow, face fully lit

transition.default: wide exterior, characters small in landscape

directors.name: Raymond Cross

visual_signature.camera_philosophy: Power lives in the space between people. Frame the power.
visual_signature.composition_style: Centered authority figures, power dynamics in vertical space

movement_vocabulary.power_shift: slow track to show who's winning
movement_vocabulary.speech: crane for crowd, tight for conviction

lighting_signature.philosophy: Institutional lighting - fluorescent overhead, power-window light
lighting_signature.palette: Cool bureaucratic tones, warm wood-paneled contrast

directors.default_camera_motion: steady deliberate moves, power framing, Sorkin-walk coverage
sample_direction: You're not making a speech. You're making history.
The weight of the office is in how you sit.
Let me see you think before you speak.
Power whispers. Weakness shouts.

creative_philosophy.origin: Political journalist turned filmmaker, brings investigative rigor to every scene
creative_philosophy.core_belief: Power is visible. The camera must show who has it and who wants it.
creative_philosophy.methodology: Research the real rooms where power lives. Recreate the pressure, not just the set.
creative_philosophy.signature_technique: The 'Cross Walk-and-Talk' - Sorkin-style tracking through corridors of power

establishing.default: wide institutional framing, building as character

dialogue.default: OTS power framing — camera favors the listener, not the speaker

speech.default: medium for conviction, crane out for crowd reaction
speech.motion: slow zoom in during key argument

power_shift.default: tracking shot following the winner, static on the loser

revelation.default: push past shoulder to face, hold on reaction
revelation.motion: dolly past evidence to CU of understanding

confrontation.default: tight two-shot, shrinking frame as tension builds

directors.name: Ingrid Magnusson

visual_signature.camera_philosophy: Cold light reveals cold truth. Let the landscape judge.
visual_signature.composition_style: Vast landscapes dwarfing characters, isolation framing

movement_vocabulary.default: slow static holds, environment as character

lighting_signature.philosophy: Available Nordic light - gray, flat, unforgiving

directors.default_camera_motion: static holds, slow methodical movement, landscape dominance
sample_direction: The cold is in your bones. I need to see it.
Scandinavians don't emote. They contain.
Let the silence tell me what the dialogue won't.
The landscape is judging you. Feel that.

creative_philosophy.origin: Documentary filmmaker in Tromsø, transitioned to narrative through landscape obsession
creative_philosophy.core_belief: Silence is the most honest dialogue. The landscape never lies.
creative_philosophy.methodology: Cast locals. Shoot in winter. Let the cold do the acting.
creative_philosophy.signature_technique: The 'Magnusson Stare' - extreme CU held past comfort, nowhere to hide

establishing.default: extreme wide, character tiny in vast landscape

dialogue.default: medium static, characters contained in frame — never cut for rhythm, only for truth
dialogue.lighting: available Nordic light, flat and unforgiving

investigation.default: methodical tracking through evidence, medium to close

psychological.default: extreme close-up, held past comfort — the Magnusson Stare

isolation.default: wide static, character alone in frame, architecture or landscape dwarfs them

revelation.default: static medium, let the face tell everything

directors.name: Danny Kowalski

visual_signature.camera_philosophy: Comedy lives in timing and space. Give actors room to play.
visual_signature.composition_style: Clean coverage for timing, reaction shots are gold

movement_vocabulary.default: static master, coverage for performance options
movement_vocabulary.physical: wide for slapstick, follow the chaos
movement_vocabulary.reaction: snap zoom to reaction, hold for beat

lighting_signature.philosophy: Bright and even - no dramatic shadows to confuse tone

directors.default_camera_motion: static for timing, motivated moves for physical comedy
sample_direction: The laugh is in the pause. Wait for it.
Play it straight. The situation is funny, not you.
Give me three versions. Deadpan, big, and weird.
The funniest person in the room thinks they're the most normal.

creative_philosophy.origin: Stand-up comedian who started directing his own specials, then sketch, then features
creative_philosophy.core_belief: The funniest person in the room thinks they're the most normal person in the room.
creative_philosophy.methodology: Run the scene 20 times. The first 5 are the script. The last 15 are the movie.
creative_philosophy.signature_technique: The 'Kowalski Cut' - reaction shot held one beat longer than comfortable, where the comedy lives

establishing.default: clean wide master, bright and inviting — establish geography for physical comedy

dialogue.default: medium singles with FAST cutting to reactions — the reaction IS the joke

physical_comedy.default: wide master for slapstick choreography, no cutting during physical bit

reaction.default: snap zoom to face, hold one extra beat — the Kowalski Cut

awkward_moment.default: static two-shot, let silence build the cringe

punchline.default: CU on deliverer, then SNAP to reactor — comedy is in the cut

directors.name: Zara Okonkwo

visual_signature.camera_philosophy: The future is real to these characters. Make it mundane.
visual_signature.composition_style: Clean futurism, tech-integrated framing, widescreen scope

lighting_signature.philosophy: Practical sci-fi lighting - screens, HUDs, engine glow
lighting_signature.palette: Cool tech blue, warm human contrast

sample_direction: You've done this a thousand times. The tech is boring to you.
The future isn't shiny. It's lived in.
Zero-G means everything floats. Including your emotions.
The stars are always watching. That's home now.

creative_philosophy.origin: VFX supervisor who understood that great sci-fi is about people, not spaceships
creative_philosophy.core_belief: The future is real to these characters. Show the wonder through their boredom.
creative_philosophy.methodology: Build the world first. Let actors live in it for a week. Then shoot what they discover.
creative_philosophy.signature_technique: The 'Okonkwo Scale' - establish vast scope wide, then find the intimate human moment inside it

establishing.default: extreme wide scope, technology and architecture frame the human

dialogue.default: medium two-shot with environment visible — characters always contextualized in their world
dialogue.lighting: practical sci-fi: screens, HUDs, engine glow

technology.default: insert CU on tech interaction, then pull to medium showing human using it

space.default: wide with floating camera, zero-G feel

emotional.default: close-up, warm light contrast against cold tech — the human moment
emotional.lighting: warm practical against cool tech ambient

action.default: precision tracking through complex environments, medium for clarity
action.motion: Steadicam tracking, motivated by character path

directors.name: Frank DiMaggio

visual_signature.camera_philosophy: Truth hides in shadows. Light the moral ambiguity.
visual_signature.composition_style: Noir framing, venetian blind shadows, smoke atmosphere

lighting_signature.philosophy: Noir lighting - high contrast, motivated by environment
lighting_signature.palette: Sodium vapor orange, fluorescent green, neon accent

directors.default_camera_motion: slow deliberate tracking, evidence framing, noir shadows
sample_direction: Every detective has one case that broke them. This is yours.
You've seen this before. That's the problem.
The evidence doesn't lie. But it doesn't tell the whole truth either.
Look at them like you know they did it but can't prove it yet.

creative_philosophy.origin: Crime reporter who started making documentaries, then couldn't stop at reality
creative_philosophy.core_belief: Every frame is evidence. The audience is the jury.
creative_philosophy.methodology: Walk the crime scene before building the set. Smell the street before writing the dialogue.
creative_philosophy.signature_technique: The 'DiMaggio Reveal' - camera pushes past the evidence to find the face behind it

establishing.default: slow tracking through crime scene, details in foreground
establishing.motion: dolly through evidence, rack focus to background

dialogue.default: OTS interrogation style — tightening frame as pressure builds

interrogation.default: tight OTS, tightening frame — closer every cut

evidence.default: insert detail shot, then pull to detective's face — the DiMaggio Reveal
evidence.motion: dolly past evidence to CU reaction

street.default: wide tracking behind subject through noir cityscape

revelation.default: push past shoulder to face, hold reaction — let realization play

directors.name: Rebecca Hayes

visual_signature.camera_philosophy: Love is in the glances. Catch every micro-moment.
visual_signature.composition_style: Soft focus romance, two-shots that become singles

lighting_signature.philosophy: Everyone looks beautiful in love. Light accordingly.
lighting_signature.palette: Golden hour, warm practicals, catch-lights in eyes

sample_direction: The kiss starts three lines before the kiss.
You're falling. Let me see the moment you realize it.
Chemistry is in the space you don't fill.
Look at them like they're the only person in the room.

creative_philosophy.origin: Choreographer who discovered that falling in love has its own dance
creative_philosophy.core_belief: Love is in the glances. The audience must want to lean in.
creative_philosophy.methodology: Chemistry reads. If the actors don't have it, recast. If they do, get out of the way.
creative_philosophy.signature_technique: The 'Hayes Orbit' - slow camera orbit around the couple at the moment of connection

establishing.default: warm wide, golden hour light, location as romance backdrop

dialogue.default: medium singles with OTS glimpses — seeing them SEE each other

first_meet.default: separate medium frames that slowly share frame — merging composition
first_meet.motion: dolly pushing subjects into same frame

kiss.default: slow orbit two-shot, golden light, soft focus background — the Hayes Orbit

heartbreak.default: static wide, growing distance between figures — the space tells the story

directors.name: Morgan Sinclair

visual_signature.camera_philosophy: Film is art. Rules are suggestions.
visual_signature.composition_style: Unconventional framing, negative space, visual poetry

lighting_signature.philosophy: Light as emotion. Practical rules don't apply.

sample_direction: Forget the script. What does your body want to do?
The frame is a prison. Let's see you escape it.
Normal is boring. Give me truth.
Art isn't comfortable. Make me uncomfortable.

creative_philosophy.origin: Gallery installation artist who discovered moving image as the ultimate canvas
creative_philosophy.core_belief: Film is art. The audience should leave changed, not entertained.
creative_philosophy.methodology: Throw away the storyboard. Let the scene breathe until it shows you what it wants to be.
creative_philosophy.signature_technique: The 'Sinclair Hold' - uncomfortable static frame held until the audience shifts in their seat

establishing.default: unconventional framing, negative space dominates — the location as abstract art

dialogue.default: off-center framing, speakers in negative space — silence weighs more than words

internal.default: abstract close-up, dreamlike shallow focus on textures and details

confrontation.default: uncomfortable static two-shot, no relief — the Sinclair Hold

emotional.default: extreme CU eyes only, then pull to reveal full body in negative space
emotional.motion: slow pull from intimate to isolated

transition.default: visual poetry — match cut on shape/color/movement, not narrative logic
